area_anchor: BRIK - simplify Brikette CI and release lanes to changed-only dev validation plus merge-only staging and main publishing
current_truth: Brikette already has CI-only test policy, an app-specific dev/main workflow, and a separate staging fast-path workflow. Changed-only validation is only partial today: core lint and unit tests are affected-scoped, but repo-global governance jobs still run and core typecheck also includes a fixed package loop. Staging and production are mostly branch-merge driven already, but the workflow surface is still more complex than the target state and still exposes manual dispatch paths.
next_scope_now: Run a fact-find that defines the simplest GitHub Actions shape for Brikette: dev runs changed-only lint, typecheck, and tests when relevant code changed; staging is a merge-only preview lane for users and agents; main is a merge-only live lane; and duplicated or non-essential workflow steps are identified for removal or consolidation.
adjacent_later: Revisit Brikette deploy-unit splitting if workflow simplification alone does not materially reduce Pages and Wrangler friction.
Generalize any successful changed-only validation pattern from Brikette to other app pipelines only after the Brikette lane is proven.
evidence_refs: operator-stated: Brikette should validate only changed code in CI, while staging and main should require nothing beyond branch merges.
why: The current Brikette CI and deploy flow is harder to reason about than it should be. The operator wants one straightforward release model: changed-only validation on dev, staging as the preview lane reached by merge, and main as the live lane reached by merge.

intended_outcome.statement: Brikette has an analysis-ready fact-find for a simplified GitHub Actions setup where dev performs changed-only lint, typecheck, and tests in CI, staging is a merge-only preview lane, and main is a merge-only production lane.
